titleStmt.title: Letter from Maurice McCartie to the Under Secretary, 25 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Maurice McCartie

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter from solicitor Maurice McCartie (1868-1931) to the under secretary for Ireland requesting the reinstatement John A. Brooks to the post of Clerk of the Petty Sessions in Killarney.At the time this letter was written Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) was serving as the under secretary for Ireland. Nathan held the position from 1914 to 1916 until he offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Killarney. March 25th. 1916 The Under Secretary to The Lord Lieutenant, Dublin Castle. Dear Sir, Re Mr.John Brooks, Petty Sessions Clerk,Killarney. I have been asked by the Magistrates attending the
Killarney Petty Sessions to forward for his Excellency' the Lord
Lieutenant's consideration the enclosed Memorial asking for a reâ
instatement of this gentleman in the office of Clerk of the Killarney
Petty Sessions. Your Obedient Servant, M McCartie
